title: Level 451
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 451: "Ümit Polat's House"}}

{{B.I.F. Theme}}{{Modern Classic Class|class=3|hexagon-color=Dark|symbol=?|box-color=Dark|hexagon-font=𝖀𝖒𝖎𝖙}}{{Fix|still needs a bit more expansion}}

{{BackgroundFitted}}



==Description ==

Level 451, from the outside, resembles an endless apartment block. It turns out this is no ordinary building, this area belongs entirely to the family known as the Polat Family. The Polat family constantly perceives travelers as 'trespassers' and a threat. The corridors narrow, lengthen, and often end in stairwells that defy logic. There is only one immutable rule here: The Polat family perceives you as a constant intruder and threat. Surrounding objects, temperature, and even sound acoustics all change instantly, directly reflecting the presence of these entities at that particular moment of aggression.Level 451 is the 452nd level of the backrooms.{{Blurimage|file=Level 451-"Ümit Polat's House".png|caption=In this image, as people take their first step onto the floor, the Family greets them from the windows.|width=330px}} The floor plan completely defies Euclidean geometry; your only guide to safety is to carefully follow the direction of the stairs.You cannot consume anything here; everything is faded in color, as shown in the image. He cannot meet your vitamin needs; feeding is impossible at this level. Only the creatures of this place can feed.It's difficult to get used to the atmosphere of this level; the weather never changes, it always stays like this. Although it's a mystery how long this level has been like this, it's thought to have existed for a very long time.The things consumed here only cause harm.It also has an atmosphere where the distinction between cold and hot is unclear because you can't tell the difference between cold and heat here; what you feel isn't that. That's why some people might say it's 0°C, but that's not it either.Normally, the level has a different name, but since the most important part is the building, the inscription on the building essentially determines the level's name; otherwise, it has a very dark atmosphere. 

==Research Report==

To: Field Researcher David Sonjack
From: Mr. Karakhanid, MEG

Level 451, known as Ümit Polat's House, appears to be an endless apartment building. When he enters, the door opens automatically and delivers a warning. There is a family portrait on the wall: Haalis, Nuriyee, Ümit, Zilaar, and Ikraa. When he looks at the portrait, he sees both their past and present forms, a mystery no one has ever solved.

{{Blurimage|width=330px|caption=The famous family portrait mentioned.|file=Polat Family Family Portrait.png}}

MEG Note: If you enter here, you find no way out. Get out the second he hears static from the television. Don't forget, the family watches him. As soon as the television starts broadcasting, the entities become extremely territorial and aggressive, so leave the area immediately. Remember: The Polat family is no ordinary anomalous group. They are an organized and unpredictable threat that exploits the absolute silence of the situation to their advantage.

Regarding Ikraa Polat in particular: When encountering Ikraa, even without eye contact, an intense coldness and a feeling of being watched emerge in the background. Haalis Polat (The Family Head): When encountering Haalis, one observes not so much a physical body, but rather a trembling silhouette, as if reflected from an old film. When he speaks to him, he ignores him and manipulates reality around him by changing the television frequency. Nuriyee Polat (The Mother Figure):When Nuriyee Polat is active in the kitchen, she distorts the time perception of objects around her. The kitchenware creates a rhythmic sound that resembles an ancient lament; this sound triggers a deep sense of regret and abandonment in travelers.

==Entity Profiles (Polat's Family Entities)==

Ümit Polat: The most dangerous and invisible figure in the family. Although there is no direct physical contact, his presence is felt in the back corridors by a sudden drop in pressure and an intense cold sensation on the back of the neck. He absolutely dislikes being talked about or having his name mentioned aloud.

Haalis Polat: The absolute authority of the region. You might find him sitting in his room in front of an old television. He's less a physical body and more a trembling silhouette, as if he's stepped out of an old video cassette. Approaching him is a direct cause for elimination; he's thought to be operating a complex mechanism that connects the television signal in his room to a scrapped vehicle outside.

Nuriyee Polat: She's constantly in the kitchen, making rhythmic sounds while washing dishes or cooking. These rhythmic sounds are so hypnotic that they evoke a deep sense of regret and entrapment in the travelers who listen. It remains passive as long as its territory is not entered.

Zilaar Polat: He is the third oldest. He is known for appearing and disappearing out of nowhere. He patrols the first floor.

Ikraa Polat: She is the youngest. She usually watches from the shadows, but sometimes stalks travelers. Making eye contact with Ikraa Polat through mirrors acts like a direct invitation to her.

== Incident Record 451-A ==
Date: June 12 2022

Subject: Unauthorized Entry and Missing Person Case

Reported by: Field Researcher David Sonjack

Summary: A wanderer named KL dropped into Level 451 from Level 11. He carried a recorder when he 

He stepped into the family den. The final audio from the device chills your blood. First comes that familiar static from the television. Then the entities whisper the same sinister words in unison: Family disperse.MEG Archive Note: We updated this file in 2026 using the data we recovered from the device on June 12 2022.

We found the device in a corridor on Level 451. A backpack belonging to the wanderer lay next to the recorder. Inside, we found an object that we believe the Polat family left behind. We also recovered a family portrait with the face of the wanderer destroyed. We have had no contact with KL since this incident. MEG officials now classify every unauthorized entry to Level 451 as high risk.

== Rules and Behavioral Patterns ==
If you break the silence rule, the television switches to white noise, and the entities start to clear the room within 30 seconds. Just like in Cendere, a sound comes from the television and makes it impossible to enter the room.

== Mental Hazard ==
Auditory Paranoia: Whispers are heard. This triggers mental breakdown in travelers, leading to acute anxiety, loss of sense of direction, and uncontrollable rage.

Belonging Delusion: Looking at family photos on the wall for longer than 30 seconds makes you feel like a missing member of that family. Travelers who fall prey to this illusion refuse to leave the level and become easy targets for the entities.

Ümit Polat's presence is manifested by a sudden drop in pressure and an intense cold sensation on the back of the neck. Immediately following this sensation, the structure of the corridors changes, trapping the traveler.

Noise Discipline: Keep silent at all times. Any noise, especially from Ümit, leads to hostile reactions. Visual Restrictions: Do not make eye contact with Ümit. Do not look into mirrors and avoid looking out of windows.

The family divides the work. Haalis handles the storage, and Ümit handles the ground floor. It is mandatory to obey the rules of every entity encountered. Different figures must be obeyed on each floor.

== Designated Safe Zones ==

=== The Attic ===
The influence of the entities reaches the roof, so what did you expect? You cannot find safety on the roof because it serves only as a basic shelter.

=== Warehouse ===
They label this area as a safe zone, but do not. Although this area is officially designated as a safe zone, momentary lapses of attention can have fatal consequences. Environmental factors in the area must be constantly monitored. Warning: Haalis Polat frequents this area, and you have zero chance of escape if you enter his radar. If the television stays on, then the storage faces danger, and you must evacuate immediately.

Level 888: This area remains undiscovered and unknown, so it has no place in the active story. The Polat family completely blocked this passage to Level 451. The family deals directly with any wanderer who attempts to use this passage, and they view you as a high-level security threat.

== Notes ==
The abnormal vehicle outside does not serve as transportation because it is linked directly to the television in Haali's room. The entities consume the organic matter despite how pale it looks. You find the entrance to the storage area marked on the stairs.

== Bases, Outposts, and Communities ==
MEG Observation Post:

MEG set up this small and temporary observation post in a secret corner on the second floor. The team left because they could not stand the strict discipline here. Now the place stands empty. The team left because of the strict discipline here, so there's nobody here now.

=== Laposte Research Team: ===
A specialized team affiliated with the M.E.G. that periodically monitors the warehouse area. Laposte's team is racking their brains trying to figure out the connection between this damn television and the car, but they haven't made any progress yet. They haven't accomplished anything.

=== No Permanent Human Colonies: ===
Don't even think about settling here; the family will never allow it. If you do, you'll only get hurt, I'm warning you.

== Entrances and Exits ==

=== Entrances ===
'''[[level 11]]:'''he find an access point inside this level. He enters the basement of an abandoned apartment building on the outskirts of the city, where someone has bricked up the windows, and this leads you directly to the corridors of Level 451. As you approach the basement entrance from [[Level 11|Level11]] , the sound of your own footsteps fades away, and the clatter of cutlery on a dining table replaces them. These sounds  and create a rhythmic resonance.

[[Level 37|'''level''' 37]]: He dives into the water, and when he surfaces, the traveler finds himself inside a kitchen faucet.

'''[[level 185]]:''' The passageway behind the tall apartment on this floor brings you here. 

'''[[level 420]]: he can reach here by walking through the snow.'''

'''[[level 445]]:''' This place can be reached by entering the paved road.

=== Exits ===
'''[[level 456]]:''' A passageway can be found here if one can access the roof from the attic floor of the building.

'''[[level 999]]:''' If you find the stairs right next to the family's main apartment, you can go up to this floor. The white noise (static) from the television becomes deafeningly loud. The moment you climb the stairs and step through the threshold, this static sound completely gives way to a profound, absolute silence that pulls you away from the house.

'''[[level 587]]:''' If one descends to the lower level of the sandcastle at this level, a passage leading to it will be found.

'''level 974:''' Listening to Cendere music and static noise from the television uninterrupted for 12 minutes is a test. If you manage to keep your sanity, he can escape this nightmare thanks to Kitty's business card that appears on the table in the room. When the whispers end with the sound of breaking glass, your transition to the next level is confirmed.

'''[[level 11]]:'''As you approach the exit to [[Level 11]] the tense static from the television in the storage area fades and yields to the muffled metallic echoes of city life. When you reach the exit point, the sound shifts into a sharp, dry click that punctuates the moment your foot hits the concrete floor of [[level 11]]

'''[[level 9]]: he''' find the corridor that contains the entrances to [[Level 11|Level11]] and [[Level 37]]. As you approach the end of this corridor, the muffled family whispers inside the building slowly give way to an eerie and distant hum from the outside.he footsteps echo in the hallway while the feeling of emptiness from the darkness and scale of [[Level 9]] clashes with the cramped nature of the house. The moment he cross the threshold, the sound cuts off abruptly, and only the wind remains.
